title: Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon

seo.title: How to Make Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon — Easy Korean Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon. This easy Korean recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Korean Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon — a beloved Korean classic

description: Kimchi Pancakes, or Kimchijeon, are a beloved Korean savory pancake, crispy on the outside and tender inside, packed with the tangy, spicy flavor of fermented kimchi. This popular appetizer or snack is incredibly satisfying and easy to make, perfect for any occasion. Each bite offers a delightful burst of umami and a hint of spice, making it an irresistible treat that embodies the vibrant spirit of Korean cuisine.

ingredients.notes: adjust as needed for batter consistency
for frying, or more as needed

instructions.title: Prepare Kimchi and Vegetables
instructions.text: Roughly chop the well-fermented kimchi into bite-sized pieces. If your kimchi is very sour, you can rinse it slightly, but typically the sourness adds great flavor. Thinly slice the yellow onion and chop the green onions. Reserve a small amount of chopped green onions for garnish.

instructions.title: Make the Batter
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the all-purpose flour, cold water, egg, kimchi juice, sugar, and salt. Whisk until just combined and smooth, being careful not to overmix. A few small lumps are acceptable.

instructions.title: Combine Ingredients
instructions.text: Add the chopped kimchi, sliced yellow onion, and most of the chopped green onions (reserving a small amount for garnish) to the batter. Gently fold everything together until all ingredients are evenly distributed throughout the batter.

instructions.title: Heat the Pan
instructions.text: Heat a large non-stick skillet or cast-iron pan over medium-high heat. Add 2-3 tablespoons of vegetable oil, ensuring it coats the bottom of the pan evenly. The oil should be shimmering hot.

instructions.title: Cook the Pancakes
instructions.text: Pour about 1/2 to 3/4 cup of the kimchi batter into the hot pan, spreading it thinly with the back of a spoon to form a round pancake. You can cook 1-2 pancakes at a time, depending on the size of your pan.

instructions.title: Flip and Finish
instructions.text: Cook for about 3-4 minutes per side, or until golden brown and crispy. Press down lightly with a spatula to ensure even cooking and crispiness. Add more oil as needed between pancakes to maintain crispiness.

instructions.title: Prepare Dipping Sauce
instructions.text: While the pancakes are cooking, combine soy sauce, rice vinegar, optional gochugaru, sesame oil, and toasted sesame seeds in a small bowl. Mix well.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Transfer the cooked Kimchi Pancakes to a cutting board, slice into wedges, and garnish with the reserved fresh green onions. Serve immediately with the prepared dipping sauce on the side. Enjoy these crispy, flavorful pancakes as an appetizer or a light meal.

tips: For extra crispiness, use a cast-iron skillet and ensure the oil is very hot before adding the batter. Don't overcrowd the pan, and add more oil as needed between pancakes.
Use well-fermented, slightly sour kimchi for the best flavor; older kimchi works wonderfully here. For a heartier pancake, you can add thinly sliced pork belly, shrimp, or squid to the batter.
Kimchi pancakes are best enjoyed immediately after cooking. If you have leftovers, reheat them in a dry skillet or an air fryer at 350°F (175°C) for a few minutes to restore their crispiness.
article: Kimchi Pancakes Kimchijeon is a beloved Korean Appetizers dish enjoyed across Korean. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
